claim: next.config.mjs line 13 sets output:"export", making the web application a fully static SPA — API routes work under npm run dev but fail at npm run build
evidence: Direct code read of next.config.mjs line 13; confirmed in SQ2, SQ4a, SQ4b, SQ5 findings independently

claim: REVISED: Hybrid fetch strategy is the primary architecture — API route during npm run dev (live MASTER_DEBT.jsonl), static JSON fallback in production. Replaces static-only approach.
evidence: API routes confirmed available in npm run dev (Turbopack); contrarian-1 identified staleness during dev as MAJOR issue; Option A-Hybrid adopted as primary

claim: BUG-01: debt-health.js line 65 uses lowercase status strings (resolved, closed) but canonical status values in MASTER_DEBT.jsonl are uppercase (RESOLVED) — filter never matches any record; breaks all web status filters and age calculations
evidence: V2-data verification: scripts/health/checkers/debt-health.js line 65 confirmed; resolve-bulk.js line 298 uses uppercase RESOLVED

claim: BUG-06: logMetricsGeneration() in generate-metrics.js (lines 335-356) writes only {timestamp, total, open, resolved, s0_alerts, s1_alerts} to metrics-log.jsonl — missing by_source and by_category from all 113 existing entries
evidence: V2-data verification: logMetricsGeneration function lines 335-356 confirmed; by_source and by_category absent from log entries but present in metrics.json

claim: BUG-03: resolve-bulk.js (492 lines) does not call sync-deduped.js after bulk resolution; no such call exists anywhere in the file
evidence: V2-data verification: resolve-bulk.js read in full (492 lines); post-resolution calls only generate-views.js (line 420) and reconcile-roadmap.js (line 432)

claim: BUG-04: TRIAGED status is not in the MASTER_DEBT schema enum — items with this status appear as unknown in web filters
evidence: Direct schema inspection; TRIAGED used in practice but absent from schema definition

claim: BUG-02: No --dry-run flag exists on resolve-debt.js or resolve-bulk.js; risk of accidental resolution (not a blocking bug)
evidence: Confirmed absent; downgraded in DECISIONS_PRE_PLAN.md after contrarian challenge review

claim: BUG-05: Shallow promotion in resolve-debt.js copies fields rather than deep-cloning the resolution object — low web impact but data integrity risk
evidence: Direct code inspection; resolution object fields may be shared references

claim: MASTER_DEBT.jsonl contains 8,472 records as of 2026-03-26; the web never writes to it — CLI is the exclusive write-side for all TDMS mutation
evidence: V2-data verification: line count 8,472 confirmed; read/write split matrix confirmed in SQ6

claim: better-sqlite3 full-rebuild strategy (DELETE + INSERT transaction) is adequate — 179ms full parse time makes incremental sync unnecessary; HOWEVER SQLite must be optional on Windows due to native compilation requirement
evidence: Direct measurement in SQ1b; contrarian-2 verified MSVC not available on this machine; prebuilt binary for Node 22 x64 Windows unconfirmed

claim: TanStack Table v8 (~15.2 kB gzipped) + TanStack Virtual v3 (~3.9 kB) is the recommended table layer; handles 8,472 rows at 60fps via row virtualization
evidence: Version compatibility confirmed; project has no existing table primitive; AG Grid rejected at 298 kB

claim: CORRECTED: Recharts and shadcn/ui chart component are NOT installed and must be ADDED. Install via 'npx shadcn@latest add chart'. Verify React 19.2.4 compatibility before committing. react-is override may be needed.
evidence: V1-codebase verification: package.json grepped for recharts, chart.js, d3, victory, nivo — all absent. No components/ui/chart.tsx file exists. contrarian-2 confirmed same.

claim: Clipboard is the V1 web→CLI mechanism — navigator.clipboard.writeText() copies /debt-runner skill command strings; 3 existing clipboard patterns confirmed. This is MVP, not final architecture; WebSocket bridge should be evaluated for V2.
evidence: Static SPA constraint eliminates filesystem writes in production; 3 existing patterns verified; contrarian-1 repositioned as V1 not final

claim: REVISED: Start with 3 discovery agents (not 7): config-drift-detector, architectural-boundary-checker, integration-verifier. Resolution-rate gate required before expanding.
evidence: Contrarian-1: 13.2% resolution rate means 7-agent runs worsen backlog; 3 agents have highest unique signal vs. existing tools; resolution-rate gate at open < 7,000 OR rate > 20%

claim: Discovery agents require individual selection menu (not all-or-nothing wave execution); token cost estimate and resolution-rate warning must be shown before running
evidence: Contrarian-1: solo developer should not be forced to run all agents; wave model is premature optimization

claim: intake-audit.js has two gaps: missing discovery_source field passthrough and missing source_type passthrough — must be fixed before discovery agents are deployed
evidence: Direct code inspection of intake-audit.js in SQ9 findings

claim: REVISED: Guided Mode is the DEFAULT entry point for the CLI (not optional); expert numbered menu available via [E] escape hatch. Mode 9 (discovery agents) is a sub-menu, not a top-level mode.
evidence: Contrarian-1: solo non-developer director; numbered menu is intimidating; guided mode should be default; OTB-2: mode sprawl management

claim: metrics.json is a hub data source consumed by dashboard tabs via useDebtMetrics() hook; cross-tab synergy infrastructure should be deferred until target tabs exist
evidence: metrics.json schema confirmed; OTB-2: Sessions (B7) has no data source, Errors (B6) unimplemented — building hooks for nonexistent consumers is premature

claim: Adding the Debt tab requires exactly 3 file changes: dev-tabs.tsx (DevTabId union + TABS array), dev-dashboard.tsx (conditional render), new debt-tab.tsx component
evidence: Tab shell architecture verified by direct code read of dev-tabs.tsx and dev-dashboard.tsx; V1-codebase verification confirmed DevTabId has 5 members

claim: The Overrides tab is the highest-ROI synergy target: override-log.jsonl entries are unregistered debt acknowledgments (DARK-02); Promote to Debt button generates /add-debt command pre-populated from override fields
evidence: override-log.jsonl schema confirmed with real data; DARK-02 identified in pre-plan decisions; zero new data sources required

claim: metrics-log.jsonl contains 113 entries spanning 53 days (2026-02-01 to 2026-03-26), sufficient for meaningful trend charts today with no new data collection — after BUG-06 fix
evidence: V2-data verification: line count 113 confirmed; total went from 868 → 8,472 items during this period

claim: 6 gaps elevated to MUST-HAVE by hybrid architecture: GAP-04 (file metadata), GAP-07 (sync freshness), GAP-08 (discovery source tagging), GAP-14 (resolution actor), DARK-02 (override-log), DARK-05 (metrics-log missing fields)
evidence: Re-evaluation of 26 gaps against web dashboard impact in SQ8 updated findings

claim: CORRECTED: known-debt-baseline.json has approximately 40 file-level entries across 3 baseline categories (raw-error-message: 1, cognitive-complexity: 22, cyclomatic-complexity: 16) — NOT 45+ flat entries
evidence: V2-data verification: file read at .claude/state/known-debt-baseline.json (66 lines); nested JSON object with 'baselines' key, 3 sub-categories

claim: nuqs URL state skipped for v1 — compatibility with output:export + Next.js 16 App Router unconfirmed; use useState instead; add URL state in v2 if need demonstrated
evidence: Contrarian-2: zero existing nuqs imports in project; three-way compatibility question not validated; solo user has no need for shareable URLs

claim: REVISED: Start with localStorage for annotations, not Firestore. Cross-device persistence is not a v1 requirement for a solo-developer tool. Migrate to Firestore if needed.
evidence: Contrarian-1: annotation volume unspecified; solo developer; localStorage is simpler and eliminates the executive summary contradiction

claim: SQLite schema for data/tdms.db: 4 tables, 25+ indexes, 10 SQL views, FTS5; cluster_primary column MUST be declared NULLABLE (2% field coverage); SQLite is optional on Windows
evidence: Schema designed in SQ1a; V2-data verification: cluster_primary has 172/8,472 items (~2% coverage); contrarian-2: better-sqlite3 native compilation risk on Windows

claim: hook-warnings-log.jsonl occurrences_since_ack field enables automatic Promote to Debt suggestion at >5 unacknowledged occurrences; sample data shows recurring warnings at 4-8 occurrences
evidence: Direct schema and data inspection of hook-warnings-log.jsonl in SQ11

claim: The Sessions tab (B7) data source is undefined — no sessions.jsonl and no Firestore sessions collection found; the debt-delta synergy depends on an undefined upstream
evidence: No session data source found in codebase or Firestore schema; B7 spec says Development session activity without a data model

claim: The Errors tab (B6) data model is undefined — no Firestore path for errors found; the error-to-debt synergy depends on B6 being built first
evidence: B6 is unimplemented (PlaceholderTab confirmed by V1-codebase verification); no Firestore errors collection path found in firestore-service.ts

claim: The Docs tab should show a documentation debt badge (982 items from metrics.json by_category.documentation) that deep-links to Debt tab pre-filtered — zero new data required
evidence: metrics.json by_category.documentation=982 confirmed; deep-link mechanism via setActiveTab+setDebtFilter props

claim: resolve-debt.yml CI workflow does not call generate-metrics.js after CI resolution; generate-metrics.js also absent from consolidate-all.js — metrics not updated when items resolved via CI
evidence: V2-data verification: resolve-debt.yml (176 lines) read in full; no generate-metrics.js call; consolidate-all.js also confirmed absent

claim: The admin claim gate covers all dev dashboard tab content — no additional auth needed for the Debt tab; Google OAuth + admin claim verified at app/dev/page.tsx
evidence: V1-codebase verification: app/dev/page.tsx confirmed Google OAuth (signInWithPopup) + admin claim check (tokenResult.claims.admin === true)

claim: known-debt-baseline.json requires a debt_id field extension to cross-reference DEBT-XXXXX items — schema extension only, not a new data source (DARK-01 reframe)
evidence: DECISIONS_PRE_PLAN.md DARK-01 reframe; direct schema inspection confirms field is absent

claim: CORRECTED: cluster_primary is a sparse field with ~2% coverage (172 of 8,472 items). SQLite schema MUST declare this NULLABLE. Web UI MUST handle null/absent values. Not a universal field.
evidence: V2-data verification: all 5 sampled items showed cluster_primary absent; grep across all 8,472 lines found 172 matches (~2%)

claim: CORRECTED: Static JSON for all 20 fields would be 7.6 MB. build-debt-data.js MUST strip fields to display-only set (~2 MB target, 68% savings). Fields to strip: content_hash, original_id, merged_from, verified_by, recommendation.
evidence: Contrarian-2: MASTER_DEBT.jsonl is 7.23 MB raw (8,472 lines, 942 bytes/record average); field-stripping to 7 default columns yields ~2.04 MB

claim: useTabRefresh hook exists at lib/hooks/use-tab-refresh.ts but is scoped to AdminTabId from lib/contexts/admin-tab-context — 10 admin components are only consumers; NOT applicable to DevTabId without generalization
evidence: V1-codebase verification: hook at lib/hooks/use-tab-refresh.ts lines 36-77; tied to AdminTabId; grep shows 10 admin component files as only consumers; no DevTabId reference

claim: MiniSearch replaces Fuse.js recommendation for client-side search — inverted index model (like FTS5) is faster than brute-force for 8,472+ records; supports boolean queries and prefix matching; ~3 kB gzipped
evidence: Contrarian-2: Fuse.js brute-force scan estimated 20-80ms per keystroke at 8,472 records; MiniSearch inverted index is significantly faster; boolean query support

claim: better-sqlite3 Windows compilation risk: MSVC (cl.exe) not available on this machine; prebuilt binary for Node 22 x64 Windows unconfirmed. Make SQLite OPTIONAL — build-debt-data.js MUST read MASTER_DEBT.jsonl directly, not from SQLite.
evidence: Contrarian-2: verified on this machine — MSVC not available; Python available; better-sqlite3 requires node-gyp which requires MSVC without prebuilt binary

claim: Pre-generated AI summaries are a Phase 3 build-time capability: build-debt-data.js calls Claude API during build to generate debt-insights.json with 3-5 natural-language insights. Cost ~$0.01-0.03 per build. Requires API key in .env.local.
evidence: OTB-1 Idea 5: architecturally sound; dataset is in memory at build time; one API call per build; fallback for offline builds; transforms numbers into actionable insights

claim: 4-phase implementation plan with independent value at each phase stop: Phase 1 (bugs + statusline S0 widget), Phase 2 (CLI expansion + 3 discovery agents), Phase 3 (minimal web dashboard), Phase 4 (web power features + discovery expansion)
evidence: OTB-2: each phase delivers standalone value; user can stop after any phase; no phase strands the user mid-build; solo operator constraint requires 1-3 sessions per phase

claim: Debt heat map / file tree visualization is a Phase 4 future capability: collapsible file tree sidebar showing debt density by directory, color-coded by severity weight. Requires GAP-04 (file metadata) fix first.
evidence: OTB-1 Idea 3: data exists; grouping logic already planned; by_file grouping mode in table design; GAP-04 is MUST-HAVE prerequisite; estimated 2-3 days UI work after GAP-04

claim: Diff view between time points is a Phase 4 future capability: two dropdowns selecting metrics-log.jsonl entries by date; shows total delta, resolved delta, S0 delta, top categories by growth/shrink. Simple version: 1 day effort.
evidence: OTB-1 Idea 6: metrics-log.jsonl has 113 snapshots; A-to-B diff is pure computation on two JSON objects; no new data source or dependency

claim: Statusline S0 widget is a Phase 1 quick win: ~30 lines of Go following D1/D5 pattern; reads existing docs/technical-debt/metrics.json; displays 'S0:26 | 13% resolved'; provides ambient awareness every session
evidence: OTB-1 Idea 7: statusline infrastructure exists; metrics.json already generated and committed; widgetHookHealth and widgetUnackedWarnings patterns are exact precedent; NOW feasibility

claim: Resolution rate 13.2% (1,116 resolved / 8,472 total) — more intake at this rate worsens the backlog. Adding 123-305 items per full discovery run while resolving ~20/week means backlog grows by ~174+ items per run.
evidence: V2-data: resolution-log.jsonl has 14 entries confirmed; contrarian-1 + OTB-2: arithmetic of intake vs. resolution rate at current pace
